qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated code execution via public prediction endpoint and custom function chatflow nodes
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding 001 identifies a whitelisted prediction endpoint (/api/v1/prediction/ in WHITELIST_URLS) that skips authentication entirely. The advisory root cause is the x-request-from: internal header branch that bypasses authorization (not authentication) for already-authenticated tenants. These are different vulnerable branches (whitelist vs. x-request-from header check) with different root causes (missing authentication on a specific endpoint vs. missing authorization on the internal-header path).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hardcoded default secrets for JWT, session, and token encryption allow authentication bypass
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding 002 identifies hardcoded default secrets (JWT_AUTH_TOKEN_SECRET='auth_token', EXPRESS_SESSION_SECRET='flowise', TOKEN_HASH_SECRET='Secre$t') as the root cause, which is CWE-798 (Hard-coded Credentials). The advisory root cause is CWE-863 (Incorrect Authorization): the x-request-from: internal header branch in the auth middleware skips authorization checks for authenticated tenants. While Finding 002 references the x-request-from header as a secondary exploit-path component, its primary root cause is credential forgery via hardcoded secrets, not the authorization bypass via header spoofing described in the advisory.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes an authorization bypass (CWE-863) via the spoofable x-request-from: internal header in the auth middleware at packages/server/src/index.ts:214, which allows any authenticated tenant to bypass all /api/v1/** authorization checks. Finding 001 identifies an unauthenticated RCE via a whitelisted prediction endpoint (different vulnerable branch: WHITELIST_URLS, not x-request-from header). Finding 002 identifies hardcoded default secrets (CWE-798) as the root cause; while it references the x-request-from header as a secondary exploit component, the primary root cause is credential forgery via hardcoded secrets, not the missing authorization check on the internal-header path. Neither finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: x-request-from: internal header branch at packages/server/src/index.ts:214 calls verifyToken without authorization checks (CWE-863).

Finding 001 root cause: /api/v1/prediction/ is in WHITELIST_URLS at packages/server/src/utils/constants.ts:12, causing auth middleware to skip entirely via the isWhitelisted branch (different branch from advisory).

Finding 002 root cause: Hardcoded default secrets for JWT, session, and token encryption at packages/server/src/enterprise/middleware/passport/index.ts:33,55 and packages/server/src/enterprise/utils/tempTokenUtils.ts:33 (CWE-798, different root cause from advisory).
